description:
Automated translation (refer to source for accuracy)

The Arima Area Water Source Forest Conservation Project, nicknamed the "Crested Eagle and Japanese Dormouse Forest Project," is a conservation initiative targeting undermanaged Japanese cedar and cypress plantations in Hanno City (approximately 1,300 hectares of mountain forest). Starting in fiscal year 2025 (Reiwa 7), the project will transition these areas toward mixed broadleaf-conifer forest, with the long-term goal of restoring natural forest and improving water source conservation, disaster prevention, and biodiversity.

Forest management activities—including thinning and deer damage mitigation—will be carried out on city-owned land in the upper Arima Dam area of the Nanguri district. The project uses two symbolic species found in the target area: the Crested Eagle (listed as Endangered IB by Japan's Ministry of Environment and Saitama Prefecture, and designated as a rare native wildlife species), and the Japanese Dormouse (classified as Near Threatened Type 1 by Saitama Prefecture and designated as a National Natural Monument). During forest work, operations will be planned with care for the Crested Eagle's nesting and hunting areas, as well as the Japanese Dormouse's food plants and moss habitat.

The project also incorporates ecotourism, monitoring, and adaptive management approaches, with a target vision set for around 2030 (five years ahead).

Source (Japanese): https://www.city.hanno.lg.jp/soshikikarasagasu/kankyokeizaibu/shinrinzukurika/13168.html
summary: ℹ️ Arima Area Water Source Forest Conservation Project ("Crested Eagle and Japanese Dormouse Forest Project")
